title: Method of delivering a therapeutic agent to a solid tumor for treatment
abstract: A method for delivering a therapeutic agent to a tumor through a target vessel includes delivering the therapeutic agent through a lumen of a catheter which has been coated or otherwise structured to reduce the wall shear stress during delivery of the immunotherapy and by generating turbulent flow during the delivery of the therapeutic agent.
claims: 1. A method of delivering a therapeutic agent through a target vessel in communication with a tumor, the method comprising: a) inserting a delivery device into the target vessel, the delivery device comprising a flexible catheter having a proximal end and a distal end, an expandable fluid pressure modulating structure fixed adjacent the distal end of the flexible catheter, an agent delivery lumen extending through the flexible catheter from the proximal end of the flexible catheter to the distal end of the flexible catheter and opening to an orifice at a distal tip of the flexible catheter, wherein the agent delivery lumen is coated or structured to be hydrophobic and is coated or structured to reduce wall shear stress during infusion; b) deploying the expandable fluid pressure modulating structure in the target vessel to generate turbulent flow within the target vessel distal to the expandable fluid pressure modulating structure; and c) infusing the therapeutic agent through the agent delivery lumen, out of the orifice of the flexible catheter, and into the turbulent flow generated within the target vessel, while the expandable fluid modulating structure blocks retrograde flow of the therapeutic agent proximal of the expandable fluid pressure modulating structure.
